arts.ttl: How Myles Lewis-Skelly took the Premier League by storm at just 18: Already the league's best passer, fixing Arsenal's fatal flaw and the trick that makes his team-mates better too
arts.sum: JAMES SHARPE: Who are you? Erling Haaland asked Arsenal's teenage debutant Myles Lewis-Skelly after John Stones had snatched a dramatic late point for Man City at the Etihad in September.

arts.ttl: CRAIG HOPE: The risk Newcastle are taking as they look to protect bumper summer budget
arts.sum: CRAIG HOPE: It is now three transfer windows without a first-team addition. A new signing would have brought freshness to a dressing-room where the elephant sits by the empty pegs.

arts.ttl: Liverpool told they only need to worry about ONE team in title race - and it's not Arsenal
arts.sum: Liverpool currently sit top of the Premier League, with a six-point lead over closest challengers Arsenal, but it is not the Gunners the Reds need to fear heading into the run-in

Defeats to Bournemouth and Fulham on Tyneside in the wake of their efficient first leg victory has manifested Newcastle's fallibility, and a buoyant Gunners are well placed to take advantage of a Magpies outfit that's shown signs of fatigue. Here's how Arteta could line Arsenal up at Newcastle on Wednesday.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's humble jibes to Erling Haaland are "good for football" claims ex-Man City star
arts.sum: Arsenal's "banter" towards Manchester City's Erling Haaland in Sunday's 5-1 Premier League win was "good for football", a former Manchester City star tells Sports Mole.

arts.ttl: Joao Felix LEAVES Chelsea as Arteta 'DISAPPOINTED' by Arsenal's business: FootballTransfers recap
arts.sum: Joao Felix LEAVES Chelsea as Arteta 'DISAPPOINTED' by Arsenal's business: FootballTransfers recap Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta has admitted that he is “disappointed” by the club’s failure to sign a new forward during the January transfer window.

arts.ttl: Howe explains why Newcastle sold Lloyd Kelly to Juventus: ‘Very reluctant’
arts.sum: Newcastle coach Eddie Howe has explained that his team were ‘very reluctant’ to let Lloyd...
Mikel Arteta has described Kai Havertz as a genetic "powerhouse" and backed the Germany international to compensate for Arsenal's failure to sign a forward in January.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta expects clarity soon over new Arsenal sporting director
arts.sum: The Gunners are yet to decide over who Edu’s replacement will be

arts.ttl: Arsenal: Mikel Arteta explains why Ethan Nwaneri is not allowed in senior dressing room
arts.sum: The 17-year-old gets ready for games away from his team-mates
